title: Create subitem on connected item
excerpt:
Hi all, 
I have a hunch this was discussed before, but I can’t find a solution :frowning:

posts.author: urir (Uri)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all,

I have a hunch this was discussed before, but I can’t find a solution

I have two tables, A and B, with a one-to-many connection (one A item can have many B items).

I want an automation that when I set a B item status to “X”, a sub-item is created in table A under the linked item. The sub-item will have its own columns and values (taken from what it has in table B).

In human-speak, let’s say Table A is employees, and Table B is Assignments. I want to be able to see a “history” of all past assignments under each employee. I want to see it in the employee table.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Instead of that, what about creating an item view (using Connected Boards) on the Employee board?

image867×673 42.1 KB

You can pick which columns to show

image1812×567 48.2 KB

All tasks could show when you look ‘inside’ each employee

image1812×567 40.9 KB

posts.author: urir (Uri)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks, but the “Assignments” and “Employees” have (almost entirely) different data, and also different goals/uses. It doesn’t make sense for me to keep all of the info in one giant board and show/hide stuff. Isn’t the whole point of connected boards to allow me that?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Maybe I misunderstood your question then? I thought you were asking to see all assignments by employee? That’s what the above does.

posts.author: urir (Uri)
posts.cookedHtml: Well I definitely misunderstood your first message, but now that re-read it, I think I might be missing something basic here. This Connected Boards option seems very likely to be a good solution, but I don’t see it. Is it an Enterprise tier thing? (we’re on Pro).

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
It’s available on Standard. If you click the + button to add a column, do you not see ‘Connected’ as an option?

image885×491 33.4 KB

posts.author: urir (Uri)
posts.cookedHtml:
I do have connected columns in the boards. They are setup and connected correctly. What I don’t have is the “Connected boards” widget.

image412×543 29.3 KB

(it’s not under “More widgets” either)

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Oh! Do you have it if you click on ‘More Widgets’ at the bottom?

posts.author: Megan345 (Megan)
posts.cookedHtml: I don’t have the Connected Boards widget either, didn’t know it existed! Are you on the CRM product or Work Management?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: We have CRM. I didn’t realize it was a product specific widget.

posts.author: urir (Uri)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks. I think Monday really dropped the ball on this. In what “serious”, paid version would a feature like this NOT be needed?

posts.author: BruceGosk (Bruce Gosk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Its available in the Item Card View (when you click on an item) - not a dashboard, or Chart view.

Bruce Gosk | bruce@mintconsulting.org

posts.author: urir (Uri)
posts.cookedHtml: In the card view I only see an option for sub-items, not connected items.

posts.author: BruceGosk (Bruce Gosk)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Uri, that is odd. Just making sure you are in the “Item Card” View - not the “Card” View?

posts.author: urir (Uri)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve added this view:

image242×248 5.37 KB

This is what it looks like:

image858×414 12.2 KB

Is this what you refer to? No connected items in it (not when you expand a specific card also), sorry. It does show connected item in one-to-one connection columns (each worker has one location, one employer), but I need to see the multiple connected items (each worker has many assignments).

posts.author: BruceGosk (Bruce Gosk)
posts.cookedHtml:
That is a cards view which isn’t correct. in your main table click on an item, and add a view there.

See this article: https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360017143959-The-Item-Card

posts.author: urir (Uri)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hmm. Finally, (sort of) progress! In that view I do have a Connected Boards widget. However, initially it doesn’t show the right connected board. The way I have set things up so far is:

In Assignments board I have a connected column to Workers board, allowing for a single select (each assignment belongs to a single worker). I didn’t add a corresponding column in the Workers board, as it doesn’t make sense to manage assignments from there.

So, when I add the Connected Boards tab in a Worker item, it doesn’t give me the Assignments board as an option to view. I then added in the Workers board a connected column to Assignments, and now I can select that board in the widget.

However… It doesn’t seem to show the Assignment items that are currently actually connected to the selected Worker item.

I tried re-creating the connected column as a two-way connection (even though I don’t want to actually manage assignments from the Workers board), and then again I don’t see the Assignment boards as an options in the widget

What gives? Why is this so hard
